# Stage 1: Critical Auth/Injection Remediation — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Patch the five unauthenticated internal endpoints, command injection in the restore endpoint, world-readable tunnel token, and add the internal secret to the example conf.

**Architecture:** Introduce a shared `INTERNAL_SECRET` in `cluster.conf` (generated by `configure.sh`, migrated by `install.sh`). The Express auth middleware splits the hardcoded `INTERNAL_PATHS` list into a small `PUBLIC_PATHS` list (no auth ever) and an `INTERNAL_PATHS` list that requires either a valid session cookie or a timing-safe-verified `X-Internal-Token` header. Node-to-node HTTP calls attach the header. Command injection in `POST /api/backups/restore` is blocked with the same archive-name regex the DELETE route already uses. The fallback `cloudflared.service` unit is rewritten to source the token from a mode-600 `EnvironmentFile`.

**Tech Stack:** Express 4, Jest + Supertest, Node `crypto.timingSafeEqual`, systemd, bash.

**Source spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-11-remediation-plan-design.md`
**Source fixes doc:** `postgresql-cluster-fixes.md` §1.1–1.4
**Stage-closing commit message (verbatim from fixes doc):** `fix: patch critical auth bypass, command injection, and token exposure vulnerabilities`

---

## Task 1: Fix 1.1a — add `getInternalSecret()` + `isInternalRequest()` helpers in auth middleware

**Files:**
- Modify: `web/src/middleware/auth.js`
- Test: `web/__tests__/auth-internal.test.js` (new)

This task introduces the helpers and the public/internal path split, but does **not** yet enforce them on route access — we add the helpers with a unit test, then in Task 2 wire them into `authMiddleware` behind the existing allowlist.

- [ ] **Step 1.1: Write failing tests for `getInternalSecret()` and `isInternalRequest()`**

Create `web/__tests__/auth-internal.test.js`:
```js
const path = require('path');
const fs = require('fs');
const os = require('os');

describe('internal auth helpers', () => {
  let tmpDir, confPath, originalCwd;

  beforeEach(() => {
    tmpDir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'auth-test-'));
    confPath = path.join(tmpDir, 'cluster.conf');
    originalCwd = process.cwd();
    // auth.js reads cluster.conf relative to its own location; we stub it via env
    process.env.PG_CLUSTER_CONF = confPath;
    jest.resetModules();
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    delete process.env.PG_CLUSTER_CONF;
    fs.rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    process.chdir(originalCwd);
  });

  test('getInternalSecret returns null when cluster.conf missing', () => {
    const { getInternalSecret } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    expect(getInternalSecret()).toBeNull();
  });

  test('getInternalSecret reads INTERNAL_SECRET from cluster.conf', () => {
    fs.writeFileSync(confPath, 'INTERNAL_SECRET="deadbeef1234"\n');
    const { getInternalSecret } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    expect(getInternalSecret()).toBe('deadbeef1234');
  });

  test('isInternalRequest rejects when no secret configured', () => {
    const { isInternalRequest } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    const req = { headers: { 'x-internal-token': 'anything' } };
    expect(isInternalRequest(req)).toBe(false);
  });

  test('isInternalRequest rejects when header missing', () => {
    fs.writeFileSync(confPath, 'INTERNAL_SECRET="deadbeef1234"\n');
    const { isInternalRequest } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    expect(isInternalRequest({ headers: {} })).toBe(false);
  });

  test('isInternalRequest rejects wrong token', () => {
    fs.writeFileSync(confPath, 'INTERNAL_SECRET="deadbeef1234"\n');
    const { isInternalRequest } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    const req = { headers: { 'x-internal-token': 'wrongwrong12' } };
    expect(isInternalRequest(req)).toBe(false);
  });

  test('isInternalRequest accepts matching token (timing-safe, equal length)', () => {
    fs.writeFileSync(confPath, 'INTERNAL_SECRET="deadbeef1234"\n');
    const { isInternalRequest } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    const req = { headers: { 'x-internal-token': 'deadbeef1234' } };
    expect(isInternalRequest(req)).toBe(true);
  });

  test('isInternalRequest rejects mismatched-length token without throwing', () => {
    fs.writeFileSync(confPath, 'INTERNAL_SECRET="deadbeef1234"\n');
    const { isInternalRequest } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    const req = { headers: { 'x-internal-token': 'short' } };
    expect(isInternalRequest(req)).toBe(false);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 1.2: Run the test to verify it fails**

Run: `cd web && npx jest __tests__/auth-internal.test.js`
Expected: FAIL with `TypeError: getInternalSecret is not a function` (or similar — the function does not exist yet).

- [ ] **Step 1.3: Add the helpers to `web/src/middleware/auth.js`**

Insert after the existing `const AUTH_PATH = findAuthPath();` line (~line 15). The file already requires `crypto`, `fs`, and `path` at the top.

```js
// Resolve cluster.conf path (env override for tests, else same resolution as app.js)
function findConfPath() {
  if (process.env.PG_CLUSTER_CONF) return process.env.PG_CLUSTER_CONF;
  return [
    path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..', 'cluster.conf'),
    path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..', '..', 'cluster.conf')
  ].find(p => fs.existsSync(p)) || null;
}

function getInternalSecret() {
  const p = findConfPath();
  if (!p || !fs.existsSync(p)) return null;
  const content = fs.readFileSync(p, 'utf8');
  const match = content.match(/^INTERNAL_SECRET="?([^"\n]*)"?$/m);
  return match ? match[1] : null;
}

function isInternalRequest(req) {
  const token = req.headers && req.headers['x-internal-token'];
  const secret = getInternalSecret();
  if (!secret || !token) return false;
  const a = Buffer.from(String(token));
  const b = Buffer.from(String(secret));
  if (a.length !== b.length) return false;
  try { return crypto.timingSafeEqual(a, b); } catch { return false; }
}
```

Then add the two new names to the existing `module.exports` block at the bottom:
```js
module.exports = {
  sessions,
  authConfig,
  setAuthConfig(config) { authConfig = config; },
  AUTH_PATH,
  loadAuth,
  verifyPassword,
  hashPassword,
  parseCookies,
  authMiddleware,
  getInternalSecret,
  isInternalRequest
};
```

- [ ] **Step 1.4: Run the test to verify it passes**

Run: `cd web && npx jest __tests__/auth-internal.test.js`
Expected: all 7 tests pass.

- [ ] **Step 1.5: Run the full Jest suite for regressions**

Run: `cd web && npm test`
Expected: all tests pass, no regressions in `server.test.js`.

- [ ] **Step 1.6: Commit**

```bash
git add web/src/middleware/auth.js web/__tests__/auth-internal.test.js
git commit -m "Add getInternalSecret/isInternalRequest helpers to auth middleware"
```

## Task 2: Fix 1.1b — enforce auth on `INTERNAL_PATHS`

**Files:**
- Modify: `web/src/middleware/auth.js` (the hardcoded `INTERNAL_PATHS` array and `authMiddleware` body)
- Modify: `web/__tests__/auth-internal.test.js` (add route-level tests)

- [ ] **Step 2.1: Add failing middleware-behavior tests**

Append to `web/__tests__/auth-internal.test.js`:
```js
describe('authMiddleware public/internal split', () => {
  let tmpDir, confPath, app;

  beforeEach(() => {
    tmpDir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'auth-mw-test-'));
    confPath = path.join(tmpDir, 'cluster.conf');
    fs.writeFileSync(confPath, 'INTERNAL_SECRET="deadbeef1234"\n');
    process.env.PG_CLUSTER_CONF = confPath;
    jest.resetModules();

    const express = require('express');
    const { authMiddleware, setAuthConfig } = require('../src/middleware/auth');
    // Simulate auth being configured so the middleware actually guards
    setAuthConfig({ username: 'test', hash: 'x', salt: 'y' });
    app = express();
    app.use(express.json());
    app.use(authMiddleware);
    app.get('/api/version', (req, res) => res.json({ ok: 'public' }));
    app.post('/api/restart/local', (req, res) => res.json({ ok: 'internal' }));
    app.post('/api/version/upgrade/apply', (req, res) => res.json({ ok: 'internal' }));
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    delete process.env.PG_CLUSTER_CONF;
    fs.rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
  });

  const request = require('supertest');

  test('public path /api/version always allowed', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).get('/api/version');
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
  });

  test('internal path rejected without header or session', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).post('/api/restart/local');
    expect(res.status).toBe(401);
  });

  test('internal path allowed with valid X-Internal-Token', async () => {
    const res = await request(app)
      .post('/api/restart/local')
      .set('X-Internal-Token', 'deadbeef1234');
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
  });

  test('internal path rejected with wrong X-Internal-Token', async () => {
    const res = await request(app)
      .post('/api/restart/local')
      .set('X-Internal-Token', 'wrongwrong12');
    expect(res.status).toBe(401);
  });

  test('upgrade/apply also requires header', async () => {
    const noHeader = await request(app).post('/api/version/upgrade/apply');
    expect(noHeader.status).toBe(401);
    const withHeader = await request(app)
      .post('/api/version/upgrade/apply')
      .set('X-Internal-Token', 'deadbeef1234');
    expect(withHeader.status).toBe(200);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2.2: Run the test — expect the new describe block to fail**

Run: `cd web && npx jest __tests__/auth-internal.test.js`
Expected: the original 7 tests still pass; the 5 new tests FAIL because the current middleware lets `INTERNAL_PATHS` through unauthenticated.

- [ ] **Step 2.3: Replace `INTERNAL_PATHS` and `authMiddleware` in `web/src/middleware/auth.js`**

Locate the current `INTERNAL_PATHS` constant (lines 54–56) and `authMiddleware` (lines 58–70). Replace them with:

```js
// Paths that never require auth (browser-facing, healthchecks, version gossip).
const PUBLIC_PATHS = [
  '/healthz',
  '/api/login',
  '/api/auth/status',
  '/api/version',
  '/api/tunnel/local',
  '/api/system/local'
];

// Paths that require a valid session cookie OR a valid X-Internal-Token header.
const INTERNAL_PATHS = [
  '/api/storage/apply',
  '/api/version/upgrade/apply',
  '/api/tunnel/apply',
  '/api/tunnel/setup',
  '/api/restart/local',
  '/api/restart/monitor'
];

function pathMatches(list, p) {
  return list.some(x => p === x || p.startsWith(x + '/'));
}

function authMiddleware(req, res, next) {
  // No auth configured — allow everything (install/setup flow)
  if (!authConfig) return next();
  // Always-public paths
  if (pathMatches(PUBLIC_PATHS, req.path)) return next();
  // Static files (login page)
  if (!req.path.startsWith('/api/')) return next();
  // Session cookie check (valid for any /api/* path)
  const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie);
  const token = cookies['pg_session'];
  if (token && sessions.has(token)) return next();
  // Internal node-to-node paths: fall back to X-Internal-Token
  if (pathMatches(INTERNAL_PATHS, req.path) && isInternalRequest(req)) return next();
  res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2.4: Run the test to verify it passes**

Run: `cd web && npx jest __tests__/auth-internal.test.js`
Expected: all 12 tests pass.

- [ ] **Step 2.5: Run the full Jest suite**

Run: `cd web && npm test`
Expected: all tests pass. `server.test.js` should still pass because `auth.json` does not exist in the test env, so `authConfig` is null and the middleware no-ops (matching pre-change behavior).

- [ ] **Step 2.6: Commit**

```bash
git add web/src/middleware/auth.js web/__tests__/auth-internal.test.js
git commit -m "Enforce auth on internal node-to-node endpoints"
```

## Task 3: Fix 1.1c — generate `INTERNAL_SECRET` in `configure.sh`

**Files:**
- Modify: `configure.sh` (around lines 236–299)

- [ ] **Step 3.1: Add the secret generation after `ETCD_TOKEN`**

In `configure.sh`, locate the block (lines 234–239):
```bash
# ----- Patroni REST API credentials -----
PATRONI_API_USER="patroni"
PATRONI_API_PASS=$(openssl rand -base64 24 | tr -d '/+=' | head -c 24)

# ----- etcd token -----
ETCD_TOKEN=$(openssl rand -hex 8)
```

Replace with:
```bash
# ----- Patroni REST API credentials -----
PATRONI_API_USER="patroni"
PATRONI_API_PASS=$(openssl rand -base64 24 | tr -d '/+=' | head -c 24)

# ----- etcd token -----
ETCD_TOKEN=$(openssl rand -hex 8)

# ----- Internal node-to-node auth secret -----
INTERNAL_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
```

- [ ] **Step 3.2: Emit the secret into the generated `cluster.conf`**

In the same file, locate the here-doc that writes `cluster.conf` (around line 285–287, the `# --- Patroni REST API ---` section):
```bash
# --- Patroni REST API ---
PATRONI_API_USER="${PATRONI_API_USER}"
PATRONI_API_PASS="${PATRONI_API_PASS}"
```

Insert immediately after (before `# --- Monitoring ---`):
```bash

# --- Internal node-to-node auth ---
INTERNAL_SECRET="${INTERNAL_SECRET}"
```

- [ ] **Step 3.3: Syntax check**

Run: `bash -n configure.sh`
Expected: no output (clean).

- [ ] **Step 3.4: Shellcheck**

Run: `shellcheck configure.sh`
Expected: no new warnings beyond any pre-existing ones.

- [ ] **Step 3.5: Smoke test in a tmp directory**

Run:
```bash
TMP=$(mktemp -d)
( cd "$TMP" && bash -c '
  INTERNAL_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
  printf "INTERNAL_SECRET=\"%s\"\n" "$INTERNAL_SECRET" > cluster.conf
  grep -q "^INTERNAL_SECRET=\".*\"$" cluster.conf && echo OK
')
rm -rf "$TMP"
```
Expected: `OK`. This validates the exact regex `web/src/middleware/auth.js:getInternalSecret` uses.

- [ ] **Step 3.6: Commit**

```bash
git add configure.sh
git commit -m "Generate INTERNAL_SECRET in configure.sh"
```

## Task 4: Fix 1.1d — migrate existing `cluster.conf` in `install.sh`

**Files:**
- Modify: `install.sh` (after the `PATRONI_API_USER` migration block around lines 42–50)

- [ ] **Step 4.1: Add the migration block**

In `install.sh`, locate the existing `PATRONI_API_USER` migration block (lines 42–50):
```bash
        # Add PATRONI_API credentials if missing
        if ! grep -q '^PATRONI_API_USER=' cluster.conf 2>/dev/null; then
            PATRONI_API_PASS_GEN=$(openssl rand -base64 24 | tr -d '/+=' | head -c 24)
            echo "" >> cluster.conf
            echo "# --- Patroni REST API ---" >> cluster.conf
            echo 'PATRONI_API_USER="patroni"' >> cluster.conf
            echo "PATRONI_API_PASS=\"${PATRONI_API_PASS_GEN}\"" >> cluster.conf
            echo "Added Patroni REST API credentials to cluster.conf"
        fi
```

Insert immediately after:
```bash
        # Add INTERNAL_SECRET if missing
        if ! grep -q '^INTERNAL_SECRET=' cluster.conf 2>/dev/null; then
            INTERNAL_SECRET_GEN=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
            echo "" >> cluster.conf
            echo "# --- Internal node-to-node auth ---" >> cluster.conf
            echo "INTERNAL_SECRET=\"${INTERNAL_SECRET_GEN}\"" >> cluster.conf
            echo "Added INTERNAL_SECRET to cluster.conf"
        fi
```

- [ ] **Step 4.2: Syntax check**

Run: `bash -n install.sh`
Expected: clean.

- [ ] **Step 4.3: Shellcheck**

Run: `shellcheck install.sh`
Expected: no new warnings.

- [ ] **Step 4.4: Smoke test the migration block logic**

Run:
```bash
TMP=$(mktemp -d)
( cd "$TMP" && bash -c '
  echo "CLUSTER_NAME=\"test\"" > cluster.conf
  if ! grep -q "^INTERNAL_SECRET=" cluster.conf 2>/dev/null; then
    INTERNAL_SECRET_GEN=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
    echo "" >> cluster.conf
    echo "# --- Internal node-to-node auth ---" >> cluster.conf
    echo "INTERNAL_SECRET=\"${INTERNAL_SECRET_GEN}\"" >> cluster.conf
  fi
  grep -q "^INTERNAL_SECRET=" cluster.conf && echo OK
')
rm -rf "$TMP"
```
Expected: `OK`.

- [ ] **Step 4.5: Commit**

```bash
git add install.sh
git commit -m "Migrate existing cluster.conf to add INTERNAL_SECRET"
```

## Task 5: Fix 1.1e — attach `X-Internal-Token` header to node-to-node requests

**Files:**
- Modify: `web/src/app.js` (`runLocalUpdate` around line 210–220, the upgrade-apply request around line 240)
- Modify: `web/src/routes/cluster.js` (two `http.request` calls around lines 238 and 262)
- Modify: `web/src/routes/cloudflare.js` (the tunnel-apply request around lines 123–134)

No Jest test for this — a Supertest test cannot observe outbound headers from `http.request` without a full mock. Coverage comes from Task 2's route-level tests (the other side of the connection) plus the manual curl gate at the end of Stage 1.

```

          const r = http.request({ hostname: node.ip, port: PORT, path: '/api/version/upgrade/apply', method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'X-Internal-Token': conf.INTERNAL_SECRET || '' }, timeout: 120000 }, (resp) => {
```

## Task 6: Fix 1.2 — archive-name validation in restore endpoint

**Files:**
- Modify: `web/src/routes/backup.js` (the `router.post('/restore', …)` handler, line 70–88)
- Test: `web/__tests__/backup-restore-validation.test.js` (new)

- [ ] **Step 6.1: Write a failing test**

Create `web/__tests__/backup-restore-validation.test.js`:
```js
const request = require('supertest');
const { app } = require('../server');

describe('POST /api/backups/restore archive validation', () => {
  test('rejects missing archive with 400', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).post('/api/backups/restore').send({});
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  test('rejects archive containing shell metacharacters', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).post('/api/backups/restore').send({ archive: 'foo; rm -rf /' });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
    expect(res.body.error).toMatch(/[Ii]nvalid archive/);
  });

  test('rejects archive with path traversal', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).post('/api/backups/restore').send({ archive: '../../etc/passwd' });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  test('rejects archive with backtick', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).post('/api/backups/restore').send({ archive: '`id`' });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  test('rejects archive with $(...)', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).post('/api/backups/restore').send({ archive: '$(whoami)' });
    expect(res.status).toBe(400);
  });

  test('accepts a well-formed archive name (passes validation, may then fail on borg)', async () => {
    const res = await request(app).post('/api/backups/restore').send({ archive: 'pg-2026-04-11_02-00-00' });
    // Validation passes -> handler proceeds, then may fail on borg spawn or conflict with a running restore.
    // The contract we're testing: status is NOT 400 with "Invalid archive".
    if (res.status === 400) {
      expect(res.body.error).not.toMatch(/[Ii]nvalid archive/);
    }
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 6.2: Run the test — the injection cases should FAIL**

Run: `cd web && npx jest __tests__/backup-restore-validation.test.js`
Expected: the `rejects archive containing shell metacharacters`, `path traversal`, backtick, and `$(...)` cases FAIL (current handler only checks truthiness).

- [ ] **Step 6.3: Add the validation**

In `web/src/routes/backup.js`, locate line 71:
```js
    const { archive } = req.body;
    if (!archive) return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Archive name required' });
```

Replace with:
```js
    const { archive } = req.body;
    if (!archive || !/^[\w.-]+$/.test(archive)) return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid archive name' });
```

- [ ] **Step 6.4: Run the test to verify it passes**

Run: `cd web && npx jest __tests__/backup-restore-validation.test.js`
Expected: all 6 tests pass.

- [ ] **Step 6.5: Run the full Jest suite**

Run: `cd web && npm test`
Expected: all tests pass.

- [ ] **Step 6.6: Commit**

```bash
git add web/src/routes/backup.js web/__tests__/backup-restore-validation.test.js
git commit -m "Validate archive name in restore endpoint"
```

---

## Task 7: Fix 1.3 — move tunnel token to `EnvironmentFile`

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/setup-tunnel.sh` (lines 52–74, the fallback systemd unit block)

No TDD test — this is a root-mutating script. Verify with `bash -n` and shellcheck.

- [ ] **Step 7.1: Replace the fallback block**

In `scripts/setup-tunnel.sh`, locate lines 52–74:
```bash
cloudflared service install "${TOKEN}" 2>/dev/null || {
    # If service already exists, update the token
    mkdir -p /etc/cloudflared
    # cloudflared service install writes a systemd unit that uses the token
    # If it fails, manually create/update the service
    cat > /etc/systemd/system/cloudflared.service << EOF
[Unit]
Description=cloudflared tunnel connector
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=notify
TimeoutStartSec=0
ExecStart=/usr/bin/cloudflared --no-autoupdate tunnel run --token ${TOKEN}
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
    systemctl daemon-reload
}
```

Replace with:
```bash
cloudflared service install "${TOKEN}" 2>/dev/null || {
    # Fallback: write a systemd unit that reads the token from a
    # mode-600 EnvironmentFile instead of embedding it in ExecStart
    # (which would be world-readable via /etc/systemd/system).
    mkdir -p /etc/cloudflared
    printf 'TUNNEL_TOKEN=%s\n' "${TOKEN}" > /etc/cloudflared/tunnel-env
    chmod 600 /etc/cloudflared/tunnel-env

    cat > /etc/systemd/system/cloudflared.service << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=cloudflared tunnel connector
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=notify
TimeoutStartSec=0
EnvironmentFile=/etc/cloudflared/tunnel-env
ExecStart=/usr/bin/cloudflared --no-autoupdate tunnel run --token ${TUNNEL_TOKEN}
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
    systemctl daemon-reload
}
```

Key differences: heredoc is now quoted (`<< 'EOF'`) so `${TUNNEL_TOKEN}` is written literally and expanded by systemd, not by bash; the token is written to a mode-600 env file via `printf`; `ExecStart` references the systemd variable.

- [ ] **Step 7.2: Syntax check**

Run: `bash -n scripts/setup-tunnel.sh`
Expected: clean.

- [ ] **Step 7.3: Shellcheck**

Run: `shellcheck scripts/setup-tunnel.sh`
Expected: no new warnings.

- [ ] **Step 7.4: Verify the generated unit is well-formed (dry simulation)**

Run:
```bash
TMP=$(mktemp -d)
( cd "$TMP" && bash -c '
  TOKEN="FAKE_TEST_TOKEN"
  mkdir -p cloudflared
  printf "TUNNEL_TOKEN=%s\n" "${TOKEN}" > cloudflared/tunnel-env
  chmod 600 cloudflared/tunnel-env
  cat > cloudflared.service << "EOF"
[Unit]
Description=cloudflared tunnel connector

[Service]
EnvironmentFile=/etc/cloudflared/tunnel-env
ExecStart=/usr/bin/cloudflared --no-autoupdate tunnel run --token ${TUNNEL_TOKEN}
EOF
  grep -q "\${TUNNEL_TOKEN}" cloudflared.service && echo "unit references systemd var: OK"
  grep -q "FAKE_TEST_TOKEN" cloudflared.service && { echo "LEAK: token embedded in unit"; exit 1; }
  grep -q "FAKE_TEST_TOKEN" cloudflared/tunnel-env && echo "token in env file: OK"
')
rm -rf "$TMP"
```
Expected: `unit references systemd var: OK` and `token in env file: OK`, no `LEAK` line.

- [ ] **Step 7.5: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/setup-tunnel.sh
git commit -m "Move cloudflared tunnel token to mode-600 EnvironmentFile"
```

## Task 8: Fix 1.4 — add `INTERNAL_SECRET` to `cluster.conf.example`

**Files:**
- Modify: `cluster.conf.example`

- [ ] **Step 8.1: Insert the example field**

In `cluster.conf.example`, locate the Patroni REST API block (around lines 45–47):
```
# --- Patroni REST API ---
PATRONI_API_USER="patroni"
PATRONI_API_PASS="replace-with-openssl-rand-base64-24"
```

Insert after it:
```

# --- Internal node-to-node auth ---
INTERNAL_SECRET="replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-32"
```

- [ ] **Step 8.2: Verify the new field is discoverable by `validate_config`**

Note: `validate_config()` in `scripts/common.sh` does **not** currently require `INTERNAL_SECRET` (the migration in `install.sh` backfills it for existing installs). The example file is a reference, not enforced. Do not add `INTERNAL_SECRET` to `validate_config`'s required list in this stage — that would break existing cluster.conf files that haven't been migrated yet.

Run: `grep -n INTERNAL_SECRET cluster.conf.example`
Expected: one line matching `INTERNAL_SECRET="replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-32"`.

- [ ] **Step 8.3: Commit (stage-closing commit — uses the fixes doc's prescribed message)**

This is the final commit of Stage 1, so it carries the stage message verbatim from the fixes doc. The fix-specific changes are summarized in the body.

```bash
git add cluster.conf.example
git commit -m "fix: patch critical auth bypass, command injection, and token exposure vulnerabilities

Stage 1 of the remediation plan. This commit documents INTERNAL_SECRET
in cluster.conf.example and closes the stage. Preceding commits in this
stage group:

- 1.1 lock down INTERNAL_PATHS with PUBLIC/INTERNAL split and timing-safe
  X-Internal-Token header (Tasks 1-5)
- 1.2 validate archive name in POST /api/backups/restore (Task 6)
- 1.3 move cloudflared tunnel token to mode-600 EnvironmentFile (Task 7)
- 1.4 (this commit) document INTERNAL_SECRET in cluster.conf.example"
```

## Stage 1 close-out

- [ ] **Step 9.1: Full regression sweep**

Run:
```bash
(cd web && npm test)
bats tests/
shellcheck scripts/*.sh configure.sh install.sh update.sh
bash -n configure.sh install.sh scripts/setup-tunnel.sh
```
Expected: all tests green; shellcheck shows no **new** warnings (pre-existing `SC1091`/`SC2034` are out of scope); all `bash -n` clean.

- [ ] **Step 9.2: Risk gate — curl verification**

The spec's Stage 1.1 gate: every `INTERNAL_PATHS` endpoint must return 401 without the header and 200 with it, and a real round-trip must work between two simulated nodes.

On a **test/dev node** (not production), with a running dashboard and `auth.json` configured:
```bash
CONF=/root/postgresql-cluster/cluster.conf
SECRET=$(grep -oP '^INTERNAL_SECRET="\K[^"]+' "$CONF")

# 1. Each INTERNAL_PATHS endpoint rejects missing header
for p in /api/storage/apply /api/version/upgrade/apply /api/tunnel/apply /api/tunnel/setup /api/restart/local /api/restart/monitor; do
    code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST "http://localhost:8080${p}")
    echo "${p} no-header -> ${code}"
    [ "${code}" = "401" ] || echo "  FAIL: expected 401"
done

# 2. Each endpoint accepts valid header
for p in /api/restart/local /api/restart/monitor; do
    code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' -X POST -H "X-Internal-Token: ${SECRET}" "http://localhost:8080${p}")
    echo "${p} valid-header -> ${code}"
    [ "${code}" = "200" ] || echo "  NOTE: non-200 may be acceptable if service unavailable; verify ${code} != 401"
done
```
Expected: all "no-header" lines show `401`; all "valid-header" lines show something that is not `401`. If any mismatch, investigate before proceeding.

- [ ] **Step 9.3: Round-trip verification (upgrade/apply between two nodes)**

If a two-node test setup is available:
```bash
# On node A, trigger a cluster-wide upgrade via the dashboard UI or:
curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-Internal-Token: ${SECRET}" \
     http://<nodeA>:8080/api/version/upgrade
# Then watch journalctl -u pg-monitor on node B — confirm the upgrade/apply call lands
# and does not return 401.
```
Expected: node B accepts the cross-node call. If no two-node environment is available, document this in the commit body and defer end-to-end verification to a pre-merge-to-main manual test.

- [ ] **Step 9.4: Final sanity check**

```bash
git log --oneline $(git merge-base main HEAD)..HEAD
(cd web && npm test)
bats tests/
```
Expected: 8 commits on the branch beyond `main` (one per Task 1-8); the last commit is the stage-closing commit carrying the fixes doc's prescribed message; all tests green.

Stage 1 is complete. Proceed to Stage 2 only after the above is green.
